-{"id":"bv-9gf","content_hash":"2a81e2e3bbb6e2cce83c62956ee152ac3304561d319fbb362479c9655d0fad17","title":"Semantic Vector Search","description":"Add natural language search via vector embeddings, enabling queries like 'memory leak issues' to find relevant results even without exact keyword matches.

## Background & Motivation
Current search is regex-based, requiring exact matches. Users want to search by meaning, not just keywords. AI agents benefit from semantic retrieval for RAG patterns.

## Value Proposition
- For Humans: 'that thing about memory leaks' actually finds it
- For AI Agents: RAG over project history - 'how did we solve similar issues before?'

## Technical Approach
1. Local embeddings (start simple: sentence-transformers via subprocess or pure Go)
2. Vector storage (sqlite-vec extension or simple flat file)
3. Incremental indexing (embed on first access, update when issues change)
4. Semantic search mode in CLI and TUI

## Scope Control
Start minimal:
- Embed title + description only
- Single embedding model
- Top-10 results
- No hybrid search initially

## Dependencies & Risks
- Embedding model choice affects quality and size
- May require external tool (Python) initially
- Could start with Go-native solution later","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:37:01.591735409Z","updated_at":"2025-11-26T23:37:01.591735409Z","source_repo":".","labels":["ai-agent","search","semantic"]}
-{"id":"bv-9gf.1","content_hash":"6463e2b7f5aff70834ede66f7dca16665fd0b734f68c090c94f2233ba7960058","title":"Research and select embedding approach","description":"Evaluate: Python subprocess (sentence-transformers), Go-native, API-based, sqlite-vec. Consider quality, complexity, performance, binary size.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:40:52.628274794Z","updated_at":"2025-11-26T23:40:52.628274794Z","source_repo":".","labels":["embedding","research"]}

Store issue_id, embedding, content_hash. Search returns top-k by similarity. +{"id":"bv-81q","content_hash":"4165b8fe5ab42625b6bb6d24759225f3ad351aa8f955106d4bb8879217cf7f27","title":"Add pkg/model/types.go validation tests","description":"pkg/model/types.go defines core data structures but has ZERO test coverage.

## Current State
- File contains: Issue, Status, IssueType, Dependency, Comment structs
- No validation logic is tested
- No edge cases covered

## Required Tests (NO MOCKS - use real structs)

### Issue Struct Tests
- [ ] Required fields validation (ID, Title must be non-empty)
- [ ] Status enum values (all valid statuses recognized)
- [ ] IssueType enum values (all valid types recognized)
- [ ] Priority bounds (0-5 range validation)
- [ ] CreatedAt/UpdatedAt consistency (updated >= created)
- [ ] ContentHash computation correctness
- [ ] Labels array handling (empty, single, multiple)

### Dependency Struct Tests
- [ ] Self-reference detection (issue cannot depend on itself)
- [ ] DependsOnID validation (non-empty)
- [ ] Dependency type validation (blocks, relates-to, etc.)

### Comment Struct Tests
- [ ] Author validation (non-empty)
- [ ] Body validation (non-empty)
- [ ] CreatedAt required

### Status Transition Tests
- [ ] Valid transitions (open -> in_progress -> closed)
- [ ] Invalid transitions detected
- [ ] Reopen transitions

## Test File Location
pkg/model/types_test.go (create new file)

## Example Test Structure
```go
func TestIssue_Validation(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name    string
        issue   Issue
        wantErr bool
    }{
        {"valid issue", Issue{ID: "TEST-1", Title: "Test"}, false},
        {"empty ID", Issue{ID: "", Title: "Test"}, true},
        {"empty title", Issue{ID: "TEST-1", Title: ""}, true},
    }
    // ... test implementation
}
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:30:40.207719514Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:30:40.207719514Z","source_repo":".","labels":["model","testing","validation"]}
+{"id":"bv-cam","content_hash":"9c6604d7c2c39fa591753229a6092635859c51dd63c8e08e309dab254ae5b528","title":"Drift Detection Test Coverage","description":"Complete test coverage for the drift detection feature without mocks/fakes, plus comprehensive e2e integration tests with detailed logging.

2. Comprehensive e2e CLI integration tests
3. Detailed test logging for debugging

- e2e tests verify full baseline to drift to alert workflow

+{"id":"bv-cam.10","content_hash":"645ab041e0ffd3f5707c5de4904405428e91e643eda4ba65dda0a328a9ea2a14","title":"E2E tests: drift config customization","description":"E2E tests for custom drift thresholds via .bv/drift.yaml:

## Custom Threshold Tests
1. TestE2EConfigHigherThresholds:
   - Set density_warning_pct: 200
   - Trigger 100% density increase
   - Verify: NO warning (below new threshold)

2. TestE2EConfigLowerThresholds:
   - Set blocked_increase_threshold: 1
   - Add just 2 blocked issues
   - Verify: warning triggered (above threshold)

3. TestE2EConfigPartialOverride:
   - Set only density_warning_pct: 75
   - Verify other values use defaults
   - Trigger 50% density (should be info, not warning)

## Config File Creation
4. TestE2EConfigFileCreation:
   - Verify drift.yaml path is .bv/drift.yaml
   - Verify YAML syntax is valid
   - Verify comments are preserved

## Logging
- Log config file contents
- Log which thresholds are active
- Log expected vs actual behavior","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:28:04.573507685Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:28:04.573507685Z","source_repo":".","labels":["drift","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bv-cam.10","depends_on_id":"bv-cam.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:28:04.585194267Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bv-cam.11","content_hash":"1b9aa80a64227e14f9688839e7d7cdbb5b9d63837cb9353fc28f73e4e8a0f641","title":"Verify 100% coverage and test documentation","description":"Final verification that all coverage goals are met:

## Coverage Verification
1. Run: go test ./pkg/drift/... -cover
2. Verify: 100% statement coverage
3. Document any intentionally uncovered code

## Test Quality Checklist
- [ ] All tests have descriptive names
- [ ] All tests have logging via t.Logf
- [ ] Table-driven tests where appropriate
- [ ] No mocks or fakes used
- [ ] Edge cases documented in test comments

## E2E Test Verification  
- [ ] All e2e tests in tests/e2e/drift_test.go
- [ ] Tests are independent (can run in any order)
- [ ] Tests clean up after themselves (t.TempDir)
- [ ] Detailed logging on failure
- [ ] CI-friendly (no external dependencies)

## Documentation
- Update AGENTS.md if needed with drift testing info
- Add comments explaining non-obvious test scenarios
- Document how to run drift tests specifically

## Final Checks
- Run: go test ./... -v to verify no regressions
- Run: go test ./tests/e2e/... -v for e2e suite","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:28:35.2085065Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:28:35.2085065Z","source_repo":".","labels":["drift","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bv-cam.11","depends_on_id":"bv-cam.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:28:35.217126316Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bv-cam.11","depends_on_id":"bv-cam.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:28:35.228812473Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bv-cam.11","depends_on_id":"bv-cam.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:28:35.235587564Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Current Coverage Gaps
- checkActionable: 77.8% - missing: actionable increase info path, blAction==0 case
- checkCycles: 85.7% - missing: empty cycle key, baseline with cycles
- checkDensity: 90% - missing: density info level (between info and warning threshold)

## Test Cases
1. checkActionable:
   - Baseline actionable=0 (skip calculation)
   - Actionable increased by 25% (info alert)
   - Actionable increased by 50% (info alert, not warning)

2. checkCycles:
   - Baseline has cycles, current removes one (no new cycle alert)
   - Both have same cycles (no alert)
   - Empty cycles in both

3. checkDensity:
   - Density increased by 25% (info, not warning)
   - Density decreased (no alert - only growth triggers)
   - Baseline density=0 (skip)

- Each test should log what scenario it's testing
- Use table-driven tests for clarity","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:24:44.694941344Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:24:44.694941344Z","source_repo":".","labels":["drift","testing","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bv-cam.3","depends_on_id":"bv-cam.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:24:44.706891736Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Edge Cases
1. Zero baseline values:
   - NodeCount=0, EdgeCount=0
   - All metrics at zero

2. Boundary values:
   - Exactly at threshold (e.g., 50% density increase exactly)
   - Just below threshold (49.9%)
   - Just above threshold (50.1%)

3. Large values:
   - Very high node/edge counts
   - PageRank values at extremes

4. Empty data:
   - Empty TopMetrics.PageRank slices
   - Empty Cycles slices
   - All counts at zero

## Test Cases
- TestCalculatorZeroBaseline
- TestCalculatorBoundaryThresholds  
- TestCalculatorEmptyMetrics
- TestCalculatorLargeValues

## Notes
- Use t.Logf for detailed logging of what's being tested
- Document each boundary condition clearly","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:25:13.457169241Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:25:13.457169241Z","source_repo":".","labels":["drift","testing","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bv-cam.4","depends_on_id":"bv-cam.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:25:13.485002864Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+{"id":"bv-cam.6","content_hash":"4409e3aad89fe5fb4e1875febb59bfb1b7224c09a4e1bb5620d98159a42eaec5","title":"E2E tests: basic drift workflow (save baseline, check drift)","description":"Create e2e tests for basic drift detection workflow in tests/e2e/drift_test.go:

## Test Scenarios
1. TestE2EDriftNoChange:
   - Create .beads with sample issues
   - Run: bv --save-baseline 'test'
   - Run: bv --check-drift
   - Verify: exit code 0, 'No drift detected' message

2. TestE2EDriftDetected:
   - Create baseline with 10 issues
   - Modify .beads to add 5 blocked issues
   - Run: bv --check-drift
   - Verify: exit code 2 (warning), blocked increase alert

3. TestE2EDriftCritical:
   - Create baseline with no cycles
   - Modify .beads to introduce a cycle
   - Run: bv --check-drift  
   - Verify: exit code 1 (critical), new cycle alert

## Implementation Pattern
- Build binary fresh
- Use t.TempDir() for isolation
- Create realistic .beads/beads.jsonl files
- Capture and log all output for debugging
- Use t.Logf extensively

## Logging Requirements
- Log each step: 'Step 1: Creating baseline...'
- Log full command output on failure
- Log expected vs actual for assertions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:26:15.097008559Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:26:15.097008559Z","source_repo":".","labels":["drift","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bv-cam.6","depends_on_id":"bv-cam.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:26:15.108973453Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bv-cam.7","content_hash":"9a0e52932bfd7d7b8c951a57c2d657fa3ccafb93c885dc2e90ad0b5789c40029","title":"E2E tests: all alert types (cycles, density, blocked, actionable, PageRank)","description":"E2E tests triggering each alert type:

## Test Cases

1. TestE2EAlertNewCycle:
   - Baseline: no cycles
   - Current: A->B->C->A cycle
   - Expected: critical alert, exit 1

2. TestE2EAlertDensityGrowth:
   - Baseline: 10 nodes, 10 edges (density 0.1)
   - Current: 10 nodes, 20 edges (density 0.2, 100% increase)
   - Expected: warning alert, exit 2

3. TestE2EAlertBlockedIncrease:
   - Baseline: 2 blocked issues
   - Current: 8 blocked issues (+6)
   - Expected: warning alert, exit 2

4. TestE2EAlertActionableDecrease:
   - Baseline: 10 actionable
   - Current: 5 actionable (-50%)
   - Expected: warning alert, exit 2

5. TestE2EAlertPageRankChange:
   - Baseline: issue-1 has high PageRank
   - Current: issue-1 removed, new bottleneck
   - Expected: warning alert, exit 2

6. TestE2EAlertInfoOnly:
   - Trigger only info-level alerts
   - Expected: exit 0 (info doesn't fail)

## Logging
- Log the exact JSONL content used
- Log baseline save output
- Log drift check output  
- Log expected vs actual exit codes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:26:38.496541468Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:26:38.496541468Z","source_repo":".","labels":["drift","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bv-cam.7","depends_on_id":"bv-cam.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:26:38.520605289Z","created_by":"daemon"}]}

6. TestE2EExitCodeInfoOnly: exit 0  
7. TestE2EExitCodeWarning: exit 2
8. TestE2EExitCodeCritical: exit 1
9. TestE2EExitCodeCriticalOverridesWarning: exit 1

## Logging
- Log full stdout/stderr
- Log parsed JSON on JSON tests

+{"id":"bv-cam.9","content_hash":"782414015c87b6fa2d70be4184655b330994668d780613f541b88288266e94af","title":"E2E tests: error handling (missing baseline, corrupt files, invalid config)","description":"E2E tests for error conditions:

1. TestE2EErrorNoBaseline:

   - Verify: error message about loading baseline

 {"id":"bv-epf","content_hash":"b2e659c9b25342975c8c67f39a6e7a2a1e8f7abdbfe0c8f78809c5b3bf629c95","title":"Multi-Repo Aggregation View","description":"Unified view across multiple .beads/ directories for monorepos and multi-service projects.

## Background & Motivation
Organizations often have:
- Monorepos with multiple services
- Microservices with separate repos
- Frontend/backend splits

Currently, bv only shows one project at a time.

## Value Proposition
- For Humans: Single view of all project issues
- For AI Agents: Cross-repo dependency visibility, scoped queries

## Technical Approach
1. Workspace configuration file
2. Namespaced IDs (repo:issue-id)
3. Aggregate loader merging multiple sources
4. Cross-repo dependency support
5. Repo-scoped filtering

## Key Challenges
- ID collision handling
- Performance with many repos
- Cross-repo dependency semantics

+{"id":"bv-kn8","content_hash":"c89e86e209773ad9055caa88552b5ef50d62a1381c3402e0e348166ca783f0c3","title":"Fix failing performance regression tests","description":"Two tests in pkg/analysis are currently failing and must be fixed immediately:

## Failing Tests
1. **TestE2EStartup_NoRegression** - large_500 project regressed to 953ms (threshold: 800ms)
2. **TestPhase1Only_LargeProject** - Status unclear

## Root Cause Investigation
- Profile the large_500 test case to identify bottleneck
- Compare against previous passing runs (git bisect if needed)
- Check if threshold is too aggressive or if real regression occurred

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both tests passing consistently
- [ ] If threshold adjusted, documented why with benchmark data
- [ ] If code fixed, regression test added to prevent recurrence
- [ ] Add detailed timing logs to identify slow phases

## Files to Investigate
- pkg/analysis/e2e_startup_test.go
- pkg/analysis/graph.go (main analysis engine)
- pkg/analysis/betweenness_approx.go (known expensive operation)

## Approach
1. Run tests with -v to get detailed output
2. Add timing instrumentation if not present
3. Profile with go test -cpuprofile
4. Fix or adjust thresholds with justification","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:29:57.978076352Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:29:57.978076352Z","source_repo":".","labels":["critical","performance","testing"]}
 {"id":"bv-lkk","content_hash":"0b85b6455a2d66914ad1621602f0cf1c3b5f88321dff750b40966024282eb8d2","title":"AI-Aware Kanban Autoprioritizer","description":"Use graph metrics (PageRank, betweenness, freshness, blocker count) to suggest priority adjustments and surface high-impact items.

## Background & Motivation
The analysis package computes rich metrics but they're only visible in the insights panel. Users and agents want actionable recommendations: 'These 5 items should be prioritized because they unblock the most work.'

## Value Proposition
- For Humans: 'Smart suggestions' that explain why items should be reprioritized
- For AI Agents: --robot-priority provides a ranked work queue with reasoning

## Technical Approach
1. Create priority scoring function combining multiple metrics
2. Generate recommendations comparing current vs suggested priority
3. CLI: --robot-priority outputs priority-ranked JSON with explanations
4. TUI: 'Suggested' badge/indicator on items

## Scoring Function
impact_score = (
    0.3 * normalized_pagerank +
    0.3 * normalized_betweenness +
    0.2 * blocker_count / max_blocker_count +
    0.1 * freshness_penalty +
    0.1 * explicit_priority_boost
)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:36:10.969534571Z","updated_at":"2025-11-27T01:03:35.76590347Z","closed_at":"2025-11-27T01:03:35.76590347Z","source_repo":".","labels":["ai-agent","analysis","prioritization"]}
 {"id":"bv-lkk.1","content_hash":"360ba65418957ce86f64c6388f5f4c07d43c8bd1ed5e64c506eaec4702b2583c","title":"Implement priority scoring function","description":"Create composite scoring combining PageRank (30%), Betweenness (30%), Blocker count (20%), Staleness (10%), Explicit priority (10%).

## Algorithm
func (a *Analyzer) ComputeImpactScore(issue *model.Issue) ImpactScore:
  - Normalize all metrics to 0-1 range
  - Apply weights: 0.3*pagerank + 0.3*betweenness + 0.2*blockerRatio + 0.1*staleness + 0.1*priorityBoost
  - Return: IssueID, ImpactScore, ScoreBreakdown map

## Staleness Penalty
- Days since update, normalized: min(daysSinceUpdate / 30, 1.0)
- Older items get slight penalty to surface them

## Priority Boost
- P0=1.0, P1=0.75, P2=0.5, P3=0.25, P4=0.0 (higher priority = higher boost)

## File Changes
- pkg/analysis/priority.go: New scoring logic
- pkg/analysis/priority_test.go: Tests with known values","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:39:56.597660662Z","updated_at":"2025-11-27T00:26:30.580840974Z","closed_at":"2025-11-27T00:26:30.580840974Z","source_repo":".","labels":["analysis","core-logic"]}
 {"id":"bv-lkk.2","content_hash":"48344babbe378eba3730f7c2cf3cf3d062d91eafec1e8ccfc11613230bf124ea","title":"Generate priority recommendations with explanations","description":"Compare impact scores to current priorities and generate actionable recommendations.

## Output Structure
type PriorityRecommendation struct {
    IssueID           string
    CurrentPriority   int
    SuggestedPriority int
    ImpactScore       float64
    Confidence        float64  // 0-1, higher when score difference is large
    Reasoning         []string // Human-readable explanations
}

## Reasoning Templates
- 'Blocks N high-priority items' (when UnblocksCount > 2)
- 'Critical path bottleneck' (when Betweenness > 0.5)
- 'High centrality in graph' (when PageRank > 0.3)
- 'Stale for N days' (when staleness penalty applied)

## Confidence Calculation
- Large score vs priority mismatch = high confidence
- Multiple reasoning factors = higher confidence
- Single weak signal = low confidence

## File Changes
- pkg/analysis/priority.go: Add recommendation generation
- pkg/analysis/priority_test.go: Test recommendations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:39:56.639885343Z","updated_at":"2025-11-27T00:31:17.416692038Z","closed_at":"2025-11-27T00:31:17.416692038Z","source_repo":".","labels":["analysis","recommendations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bv-lkk.2","depends_on_id":"bv-lkk.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:40:11.711767711Z","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bv-lkk.3","content_hash":"32eb436365d02ac3686537e9e32d7fb7548f3f4990a7290f62d8a31412372dba","title":"Add --robot-priority CLI flag","description":"Output priority recommendations as JSON for AI agents and scripts.

## Flag Definition
robotPriority := flag.Bool('robot-priority', false, 'Output priority recommendations as JSON')

## Output Format
{
  'recommendations': [{
    'id': 'issue-123',
    'current_priority': 2,
    'suggested_priority': 0,
    'impact_score': 0.85,
    'confidence': 0.9,
    'reasoning': ['Blocks 5 high-priority items', 'Critical path bottleneck']
  }],
  'summary': {
    'total_issues': 50,
    'recommendations': 8,
    'high_confidence': 3
  }
}

## Sorting
- Sort by confidence DESC, then impact_score DESC
- Top issues are most confident recommendations

+{"id":"bv-qfr","content_hash":"711fb5debc900f45f20446792af4882403ab9740d3dcc99bdc8bc521577b212b","title":"Comprehensive Test Coverage Overhaul","description":"Achieve complete unit test coverage without mocks/fakes and comprehensive e2e integration tests with detailed logging across all packages.

## Current State Analysis
- **Overall Coverage**: ~75-85% across packages, but highly variable
- **Critical Gaps**: 
  - pkg/ui at only 52.5% (model.go is 71KB with only 6 tests)
  - pkg/model/types.go has 0% coverage
  - E2E suite has only 2 basic tests
- **Failing Tests**: 2 performance regression tests in pkg/analysis
- **Missing**: Detailed test logging, CI coverage reporting

## Goals
1. **Unit Tests**: 90%+ coverage on all packages using real implementations (no mocks)
2. **E2E Tests**: Comprehensive integration tests covering all CLI flags and workflows
3. **Logging**: Detailed, structured logging in all tests for debugging
4. **CI Integration**: Coverage reporting, thresholds, and badges

## Philosophy
- Test real behavior, not mocked behavior
- Tests should be documentation of expected behavior
- Failures should be immediately diagnosable from logs
- Performance tests must have stable, meaningful baselines

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All packages ≥90% statement coverage (UI ≥85% acceptable)
- [ ] Zero failing tests in CI
- [ ] E2E tests cover all --robot-* flags
- [ ] Test output includes timing, context, and clear failure messages
- [ ] Coverage badge in README showing ≥85%","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-30T15:29:36.312582996Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:29:36.312582996Z","source_repo":".","labels":["coverage","quality","testing"]}
 {"id":"bv-qjc","content_hash":"0d4b8d0db1b0106247025b984fc17431936bc0204245e8bbf7b3ffb6db9ab495","title":"Actionable Exports & Hooks System","description":"Enhance exports to include runnable commands and add a hook system for automation.

## Background & Motivation
Current exports are read-only reports. Users want exports that enable action: 'here's the command to close these resolved issues' or 'here's the PR description for this work'.

## Value Proposition
- For Humans: Exports include copy-paste ready commands
- For AI Agents: Chain exports → actions without manual parsing

## Technical Approach
1. Enhance markdown export with bd command snippets
2. Add hook system (pre-export, post-export)
3. Hook configuration in .bv/hooks.yaml
4. Environment variables passed to hooks

## Hook Use Cases
- Post to Slack after export
- Create GitHub issue from bead
- Trigger CI pipeline
- Sync to external tracker

## Security Considerations
- Hooks are opt-in (require explicit config)
- Commands are not auto-executed
- Hook output captured but not interpreted","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:36:37.178866162Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T14:47:06.583669217Z","closed_at":"2025-11-30T14:47:06.583669217Z","source_repo":".","labels":["automation","export","hooks"]}
 {"id":"bv-qjc.1","content_hash":"130744b0eeb0e3704855bc38688f4baaab984cb264f09c1ff8d691ec043b790f","title":"Add command snippets to markdown export","description":"Include ready-to-run bd commands in markdown exports.

## Export Enhancement
Add 'Quick Actions' section to markdown export:

### Quick Actions
```bash
# Close all resolved items in this export
bd close issue-1 issue-2 issue-3

# Bulk priority update
bd update issue-4 issue-5 -p P1
```

## Per-Issue Actions
Each issue card includes:
- bd close <id>: Mark as done
- bd comment <id> '<text>': Add comment
- bd update <id> -p <priority>: Change priority

## Implementation
- Add CommandSnippets field to export.Options
- Generate commands based on current state
- Use proper escaping for shell safety

## File Changes
- pkg/export/markdown.go: Add command generation
- pkg/export/markdown_test.go: Test command output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:40:24.899902692Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T05:03:42.090649656Z","closed_at":"2025-11-30T05:03:42.090657856Z","source_repo":".","labels":["automation","export"]}
 {"id":"bv-qjc.2","content_hash":"6516a7fa4ea412b173f2aab73a47262d12d327023859d39e503236fce6ebc16f","title":"Design and implement hook configuration system","description":"Design and implement hook configuration system.

## Configuration File
.bv/hooks.yaml:
```yaml
hooks:
  pre-export:
    - name: validate-issues
      command: './scripts/validate.sh'
      timeout: 10s
  post-export:
    - name: slack-notify
      command: 'curl -X POST ...'
      timeout: 30s
      env:
        SLACK_WEBHOOK: ${SLACK_WEBHOOK}
```

## Environment Variables Passed to Hooks
- BV_EXPORT_PATH: Output file path
- BV_EXPORT_FORMAT: 'markdown' or 'json'
- BV_ISSUE_COUNT: Number of issues exported
- BV_TIMESTAMP: Export timestamp

## Hook Execution
- 30s default timeout, configurable per hook
- Failures logged but don't break export (unless pre-export)
- pre-export failure cancels export
- Stdout/stderr captured and displayed

## File Changes
- pkg/hooks/config.go: Configuration types and loader
- pkg/hooks/executor.go: Hook execution logic
- pkg/hooks/executor_test.go: Tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:40:24.939948976Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T07:39:47.503672594Z","closed_at":"2025-11-30T07:39:47.503672594Z","source_repo":".","labels":["configuration","hooks"]}
